description:
A solar sales representative needs to determine the DC system capacity for a commercial rooftop PV installation in Tucson, AZ (32.22°N, -110.93°W) that will produce approximately 120,000 kWh of AC energy per year.

System Configuration:
- Location: Tucson, AZ (32.22°N, -110.93°W)
- Array type: Fixed open rack
- Tilt: 32 degrees
- Azimuth: 180 degrees (south-facing)
- DC to AC ratio: 1.2
- System losses: 14.08%
- Module type: Standard

Determine the DC capacity (kW) that produces closest to 120,000 kWh/year using PySAM's Pvwattsv8 module or SAM GUI with TMY weather data for Tucson.

Save the results as a JSON file at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tucson_sizing_result.json with the following keys:
- "system_capacity_kw": optimal DC system size in kW (numeric)
- "annual_energy_kwh": annual AC energy at that size (numeric)
- "capacity_factor_pct": capacity factor as percentage (numeric)
- "monthly_energy_kwh": array of 12 monthly energy values in kWh (numeric array)
- "target_energy_kwh": 120000
- "location": "Tucson, AZ"
- "tilt_deg": 32
- "azimuth_deg": 180

Note: SAM bundled weather data may be in the directory listed in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt. You can also use PySAM to fetch NSRDB data or use any available Arizona TMY file if Tucson specifically is unavailable.
